name: Rhode Island
tagline: A small, centralized state moving fast: a Workday ERP cutover, a post-RIBridges security rebuild, a just-released statewide AI roadmap, and $108.7M in BEAD broadband — all routed through one IT division and one purchasing office.

execSummary.summary: Rhode Island is one of the most centralized technology buyers in the country: a single division (the Division of Enterprise Technology Strategy & Services, ETSS, under the Department of Administration) sets IT strategy for the executive branch, and a single Division of Purchases runs statewide Master Price Agreements through the Ocean State Procures (OSP) eProcurement system. Three dynamics define the current moment for a Dell seller. First, ETSS is in a heavy modernization push — it completed 114 projects in 2025 (up from 46), including a Workday-based ERP cutover that retired aging mainframe systems. Second, the 2024 RIBridges data breach triggered a sustained security rebuild (Splunk, firewall, and VPN upgrades) that keeps cybersecurity at the top of the agenda. Third, McKee's AI Task Force released its statewide AI roadmap in January 2026, and ETSS is rolling out Microsoft Copilot Chat across executive agencies. The buying motion is simple to map but contract-gated — Dell coverage on the right MPAs and registration in OSP is the price of entry.
opportunities: Infrastructure and endpoint refresh tied to ETSS modernization (ERP cutover retired mainframe systems; ~4,500 PCs configured in 2025).
Cybersecurity rebuild after the RIBridges breach — secure infrastructure, logging/SIEM-adjacent compute, and managed services.
AI enablement as the state operationalizes its Jan 2026 roadmap and the DAAI Center of Excellence — governed, private AI for sensitive state data.
Higher-ed and research compute at URI, Brown, and RIC's new cybersecurity institute as independent AI-infrastructure buyers.

budget.intro: Rhode Island budgets annually; the fiscal year begins July 1. Governor McKee signed a $15.2B FY2027 budget on June 12, 2026 — the largest in state history, roughly $300M above his January proposal and ~$900M above FY2026. As in most states, there is no single consolidated 'state IT budget' line — technology spend sits inside ETSS operations and is distributed across agency programs. ETSS itself manages a large contract portfolio: it reported roughly $154M in contract value and ~$5.3M in negotiated vendor savings in 2025.

stats.label: Medicaid/SNAP IT
stats.note: IT + staff for federal benefit changes

breakdown.label: Medicaid/SNAP IT (FY27 request)
breakdown.note: IT, personnel, resources for federal changes

notes: Rhode Island uses an annual (not biennial) budget cycle; figures are single-year.
Broadband dollars are largely federal pass-through administered by RI Commerce, not ETSS — confirm program ownership before forecasting.
There is no published consolidated state IT line; technology spend is embedded across ETSS and agency budgets.

FY2027 (governor's proposed, per NASBO): $5.95B general fund, +2.5% vs FY2026; $14.86B all funds. Thin free surplus ($0.8M) with a $307M Budget and Cash Stabilization Fund balance; proposes IT and staff investment to administer Medicaid and SNAP under OBBBA.

initiatives.name: ETSS Modernization & Workday ERP
initiatives.summary: ETSS more than doubled completed projects in 2025 (114 vs. 46), headlined by a Workday-based ERP that retired aging mainframe systems — finance went live July 2025, payroll/HCM in November, with hyper-care support running into early 2026. A modernization wave at this scale drives infrastructure, endpoint, and lifecycle demand across executive agencies.

initiatives.name: Post-RIBridges Cybersecurity Rebuild
initiatives.summary: Following the 2024 RIBridges data breach, the state upgraded its security stack — deploying Splunk to replace legacy logging and hardening the RIBridges firewall and VPN. Security remains a board-level priority and a durable opening for secure infrastructure, resilient compute, and managed services.

initiatives.name: AI Task Force Roadmap & DAAI Center of Excellence
initiatives.summary: EO 24-06 created an AI Task Force (chaired by former Rep. Jim Langevin) and a Data, Analytics & AI Center of Excellence (DAAI CoE) inside ETSS. The Task Force's statewide roadmap, released January 2026, emphasizes government AI pilots, workforce upskilling, and responsible adoption over heavy regulation. ETSS is rolling Microsoft Copilot Chat across executive agencies — a foundation for governed AI on state data.

initiatives.name: Internet for All / BEAD Broadband
initiatives.summary: Rhode Island holds a $108.7M BEAD allocation (administered by RI Commerce) plus a ~$52M Capital Projects Fund broadband effort, targeting fiber to the large majority of eligible locations. Primarily a connectivity buildout, but the network/edge and downstream digital-services demand are worth tracking.

procurement.intro: Rhode Island runs a tightly centralized model. The Division of Enterprise Technology Strategy & Services (ETSS) — within the Department of Administration — owns executive-branch IT strategy, operations, and most technology projects. The Division of Purchases (RIDOP) is the statewide procurement authority; technology buys typically run through Master Price Agreements (MPAs) competed for the whole state, with many open to municipalities, higher-ed, and quasi-public entities. All solicitations and awards flow through the Ocean State Procures (OSP) eProcurement platform.

vehicles.name: Master Price Agreements (MPAs)
vehicles.detail: Primary statewide vehicle. Pre-competed by the Division of Purchases; many carry statewide applicability so municipalities and higher-ed can buy off them. Confirm Dell and reseller coverage on the relevant MPAs.

vehicles.name: Ocean State Procures (OSP)
vehicles.detail: The state's eProcurement system for vendor registration, solicitations, and awards — registration is a prerequisite to bid.

vehicles.name: Cooperative / consortium agreements
vehicles.detail: RIDOP also leverages external purchasing consortia for pricing; verify which cooperative vehicles Dell can sell through in RI.

entities.name: ETSS (Dept. of Administration)
entities.role: Central IT strategy, operations, and project delivery for the executive branch — the primary technology relationship.

entities.name: Division of Purchases (RIDOP)
entities.role: Statewide procurement authority; owns MPAs and the OSP platform.

entities.name: RI Commerce
entities.role: Administers BEAD and broadband programs — separate buyer from ETSS.

entities.name: Higher education (URI, Brown, RIC)
entities.role: Independent research-computing and AI-infrastructure buyers; RIC hosts a new cybersecurity institute.

procurement.competition: Rhode Island is a small, centralized, relationship-driven market. With one IT division and one purchasing office, deals hinge on being on the right MPAs and aligning to ETSS's active priorities — modernization, post-breach security, and AI enablement — rather than competing purely on price. Incumbents with established MPA coverage have an edge; differentiation comes from secure infrastructure, AI-ready compute, and services that map to ETSS's published roadmap.

powerStructure.name: Daniel McKee
powerStructure.title: Governor
powerStructure.note: Signed the $15.2B FY2027 budget June 12, 2026; issued EO 24-06 standing up the AI Task Force and DAAI Center of Excellence. Political-continuity risk: trailing in the Sept 8, 2026 Democratic primary and denied the state party endorsement June 20, 2026 — a new administration could reshuffle ETSS/IT leadership in 2027.

powerStructure.name: Brian Tardiff
powerStructure.title: Chief Digital Officer & State CIO
powerStructure.note: Leads ETSS; called 2025 an 'unprecedented' year of project delivery.

powerStructure.name: Nathan "Nate" Loura
powerStructure.title: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)
powerStructure.note: State CISO leading the post-RIBridges security program; 22-year RI Air National Guard veteran. Public-source LinkedIn/event listings; confirm current title directly before engaging.

powerStructure.name: Scott Gausland
powerStructure.title: Lead, Data/Analytics/AI Center of Excellence
powerStructure.note: Runs the state's innovation hub for data, analytics, and AI projects.

powerStructure.name: Jim Langevin
powerStructure.title: Chair, AI Task Force
powerStructure.org: RIC Institute for Cybersecurity & Emerging Technologies
powerStructure.note: Former U.S. Representative and cyber leader; shaped the Jan 2026 AI roadmap.

news.title: RI Democratic Party declines to endorse McKee at June 20 convention
news.impact: Split convention vote (McKee 81, Foulkes 75, short of the 84 needed) left the gubernatorial race unendorsed — a first for a sitting governor. With Foulkes leading polls and local committees ahead of the Sept 8/9 primary, a 2027 administration change could reshuffle ETSS/DOA leadership. Relationship-continuity risk to monitor.

news.title: Lawmakers introduce post-RIBridges cybersecurity bill (2026-S 2638 / H 7509)
news.impact: Sen. Victoria Gu and Rep. Lauren Carson's bill would modernize the 2015 Identity Theft Protection Act — requiring risk-based security programs aligned to recognized frameworks (e.g., NIST CSF) and timely breach notification to ETSS. Status pending as of late June; if enacted, raises the compliance/security baseline for agencies and vendors.

news.title: Gov. McKee signs $15.2B FY2027 budget — largest in state history
news.impact: Highest budget ever (~$900M above FY2026); includes ~$20M for IT, personnel, and resources to keep Rhode Islanders enrolled in Medicaid/SNAP amid federal changes — an eligibility-systems modernization angle. FY begins July 1, 2026.

news.title: ETSS more than doubles completed projects in 2025 (114 vs. 46)
news.impact: Signals a sustained modernization wave — infrastructure, endpoint, and lifecycle demand across executive agencies.

news.title: Rhode Island releases its statewide AI roadmap
news.impact: Task Force prioritizes government AI pilots and upskilling over heavy regulation — an opening for governed, private AI.

news.title: Gov. McKee proposes ~$14.9B FY2027 budget (incl. $19.3M IT for Medicaid/SNAP)
news.impact: Tech spend embedded in agency programs; benefit-systems IT is a funded near-term need.

dellActions: Anchor the relationship at ETSS: map Dell infrastructure, endpoint, and lifecycle offerings to the post-ERP modernization wave and the ~4,500-PC-per-year refresh cadence.
Lead with security after RIBridges — position resilient/secure compute and managed services that complement the state's Splunk and network hardening work.
Bring a governed-AI motion to the DAAI Center of Excellence: private/on-prem AI for sensitive state data as the Jan 2026 roadmap and Copilot rollout mature.
Confirm Dell and reseller coverage on the relevant statewide MPAs and ensure registration is current in Ocean State Procures (OSP).
Develop higher-ed and research accounts (URI, Brown, RIC's cybersecurity institute) as independent AI-infrastructure buyers alongside the executive branch.
